questions.question: Explain the purpose of the key parameter in System V IPC functions.
expectedPoints: Used to identify IPC objects uniquely
Can be generated using ftok() function
IPC_PRIVATE creates a new object guaranteed to be unique
Allows unrelated processes to access the same IPC object

questions.question: Compare and contrast the three System V IPC mechanisms, discussing their primary purposes, advantages, and typical use cases.
expectedPoints: Message Queues: Structured message passing, persistent storage, suitable for ordered communication
Semaphores: Synchronization primitive, counter-based, used for mutual exclusion and coordination
Shared Memory: Highest performance IPC, direct memory access, requires external synchronization
Persistence: All three persist beyond process lifetime until explicitly removed
Access control: All use similar permission structures based on Unix file permissions
Use cases: Message queues for structured data exchange, semaphores for synchronization, shared memory for high-speed data sharing
